display.purpose: Board-level title only. Appears once per view.

headline.purpose: Section headings. h2 in UI context.

body.purpose: Primary reading text. 65–75ch max line length for prose.

label.purpose: Column headers and structural labels. Uppercase. Never for user-generated content.

mono.purpose: Code snippets and counters. Always on code-bg.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Near-invisible card definition. Insurance on displays where #fff/#ebecf0 reads as identical.

shadows.name: card-drag
shadows.purpose: Active drag state. The elevation jump signals the card has lifted off the surface.

shadows.name: ambient-dark
shadows.purpose: Dark mode variant of ambient shadow.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transitions. Enter and exit.

motion.name: ease-out-fast
motion.purpose: Card drop and form reveal. Element arriving at rest.

motion.name: duration-micro
motion.purpose: Button hover, focus ring. Immediate feedback.

motion.name: duration-short
motion.purpose: Card form reveal, shadow transition on drag start.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Font-size step down (18px → 16px), h1 step down (56px → 36px).

components.name: Kanban Card
components.description: Draggable task unit. Title only in MVP. Lifts on drag.
components.html: Fix login redirect on mobile
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 4px; padding: 10px 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(9, 30, 66, 0.15); cursor: grab; user-select: none; } .ds-card:focus { outline: 2px solid #0052cc; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 3px 8px rgba(9, 30, 66, 0.2); transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.4; color: #172b4d; margin: 0; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-card:hover { transition: none; } }

components.name: Kanban Column
components.description: Fixed-width (280px) column container. Structural, not interactive.
components.html:
To Do

Write release notes
Update docs
components.css: .ds-column { background: #ebecf0; border-radius: 6px; padding: 12px; width: 280px; min-height: 200px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-column-header { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #5e6c84; margin: 0 0 12px; } .ds-column-cards { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; min-height: 60px; } .ds-card-in-column { background: #fff; border-radius: 4px; padding: 10px 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(9,30,66,0.15); font-size: 0.9rem; color: #172b4d; } .ds-add-card-btn { width: 100%; margin-top: 8px; padding: 6px 8px; background: transparent; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #5e6c84; cursor: pointer; text-align: left; } .ds-add-card-btn:hover { background: rgba(9,30,66,0.06); color: #172b4d; } .ds-add-card-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0052cc; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Add Card Form
components.description: Inline card-creation form inside a column. Replaces the Add card button.
components.css: .ds-card-form { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; margin-top: 8px; } .ds-card-input { width: 100%; padding: 8px 10px; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e5e4e7; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.9rem; color: #172b4d; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-card-input:focus { outline: 2px solid #0052cc; outline-offset: 1px; border-color: transparent; } .ds-card-form-actions { display: flex; gap: 6px; } .ds-btn-submit { padding: 6px 16px; background: #aa3bff; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms; } .ds-btn-submit:hover { background: #9528e6; } .ds-btn-submit:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #aa3bff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-cancel { padding: 6px 16px; background: transparent; color: #6b6375; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-cancel:hover { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.05); } .ds-btn-cancel:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0052cc; outline-offset: 2px; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-submit { transition: none; } }

components.name: Activity Feed Entry
components.description: Append-only event log item. System-generated, never user-styled.
components.html: • Card moved to In Progress2m ago
components.css: .ds-activity-entry { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; padding: 4px 0; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #6b6375; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e4e7; } .ds-activity-entry:last-child { border-bottom: none; } .ds-activity-action strong { color: #08060d; font-weight: 500; } .ds-activity-time { font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #8a849a; white-space: nowrap; margin-left: 12px; }

narrative.overview:
BanyanBoard is a workspace that earns trust by disappearing. The interface is sparse and intentional — every element present because it reduces the next drag, not because it fills space. The visual vocabulary is inherited from familiar tools (system fonts, standard controls, Atlassian-adjacent board colors) on purpose: novelty would only slow the user down.

The color palette is muted by design. Signal Violet appears on actions and focus states only, never decoration. The board's grays (#f4f5f7 → #ebecf0 → #ffffff) read as a natural depth stack — column lighter than board background, card lighter than column — without any shadows at rest. The system's restraint is its personality.

This system explicitly rejects the Jira pattern: no dense chrome, no sidebar trees, no configuration forms masquerading as collaboration.
keyCharacteristics: Muted neutral ground, one functional accent
System font stack — familiar, not designed
Shadows only on interaction, never at rest
Focus states are explicit and unmissable

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Signal Violet appears on ≤2 interactive elements per screen. If you find yourself reaching for it as a background, a badge fill, or a section header — stop. That color's power is its scarcity.

rules.name: The Tonal Stack Rule
rules.body: Board → Column → Card is a lightness sequence: #f4f5f7 → #ebecf0 → #ffffff. Do not break this order. A column whiter than a card destroys the spatial model.

rules.name: The System Stack Rule
rules.body: Never replace the system font stack with a webfont without updating --sans, --heading, and the frontmatter typography tokens simultaneously. One-off font-family overrides create an uncanny valley between the token system and the rendered UI.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A card that looks lifted before you touch it has lost the ability to signal drag state. Elevation is reserved for things that move.

dos: Do keep Signal Violet to focus states, primary action buttons, and hover accents only. One accent color per screen maximum.
Do use the tonal stack (board → column → card: #f4f5f7 → #ebecf0 → #ffffff) to convey depth. Never reverse it.
Do apply Focus Blue (#0052cc) for keyboard focus rings — it contrasts with both Signal Violet and the neutral surfaces.
Do keep shadows out of resting states. Shadows are state signals: drag, hover-lift, popover. Not decoration.
Do use the system font stack (system-ui, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif). Skip webfonts; they add latency and brand none.
Do keep card UI to title + drag affordance. Every field added to a card increases cognitive load by more than its information value.
Do test color contrast: body text (#6b6375 on #fff) is 4.9:1 — on the threshold. Never lighten body text further.
Do provide a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every drag animation and state transition.
donts: Don't add features that require reading docs. If a UI pattern needs explaining, the pattern is wrong.
Don't make this feel like Jira. No sidebar trees, no configuration forms, no dense chrome.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards, column headers, or callouts.
Don't use gradient text or background-clip: text with a gradient.
Don't add decorative motion. Transitions exist to convey state change only.
Don't invent new status colors beyond warning amber. Encode state in the column, not the card color.
Don't use modal dialogs as first thought. Card creation is inline; keep it that way.
Don't add eyebrows, section kickers, or numbered section markers. The board is a tool surface.
Don't break the 1126px container by adding full-bleed sections or edge-to-edge backgrounds.
